query: Humane AI Pin server shutdown date bricked device hack status

{"query":"Humane AI Pin server shutdown date bricked device hack status","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1710,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://mashable.com/article/humane-ai-pin-discontinued-killed","title":"Humane has killed its Ai Pin less than a year after its release | Mashable","content":"Humane's Ai Pin is about to become an expensive brick, with the startup set to discontinue service less than a year after the wearable AI gadget began shipping.
In a post to Humane's support website, the company announced that all of its Ai Pins will stop working in just 10 days.
All Ai Pins will stop connecting to Humane's servers on Feb.
28, and all customer data will be permanently deleted as well, with users instructed to download anything they don't want to lose before then.
"Humane’s AI platform Cosmos, backed by an incredible group of engineers, will help us create an intelligent ecosystem across all HP devices from AI PCs to smart printers and connected conference rooms," HP's President of Technology and Innovation Tuan Tran said in a statement."},{"url":"https://complexdiscovery.com/the-end-of-humane-ai-pin-hps-strategic-shift-toward-ai-integration/","title":"The End of Humane AI Pin: HP’s Strategic Shift Toward AI Integration","content":"As HP reshapes Humane’s assets into a more cohesive AI strategy, this development underscores the importance of aligning cutting-edge technology with practical, user-focused applications.
The Humane AI Pin, a wearable device created by ex-Apple designers Imran Chaudhri and Bethany Bongiorno, will permanently shut down its functionality by February 28, 2025, at 12 PM PT.
The shutdown follows HP’s acquisition of Humane for $116 million—a sharp drop from previous valuations that was rumored to reach as high as $1 billion.
The shutdown aligns with HP’s strategic shift toward strengthening its AI ecosystem.
Refunds are being offered only to customers who purchased the device after November 15, 2024, with a request deadline of February 27, leaving many early adopters frustrated by the narrow refund policy.
Learn more at ComplexDiscovery.com."},{"url":"https://www.designboom.com/technology/humane-ai-pin-debut-wearable-screenless-smartphone-flash-apps-hands-11-09-2023/","title":"humane AI pin shuts down and discontinues all its features","content":"UPDATE (February 19th, 2025): Humane AI Pin announces it is shutting down and discontinuing all of its features, including calling, messaging, AI queries and responses, and cloud access.
Less than two years after its debut, the hands-free device that has hoped to become a smartphone substitute no longer works.
In a company note, the team says that every Humane AI Pin will officially stop working or operating starting February 28th, 2025, at 12pm PST.
On the date mentioned, the hands-free device stops connecting to Humane’s servers as its access fully retires.
In other news, Humane sells its AI-powered platform Cosmos and intellectual property with more than 300 patents and patent applications to HP.
Another feature is Catch Me Up where the wearable device reads through all the activities and reminders that the user has to finish and get done, acting like an assistant.
Chaudhri spent over 20 years at Apple helping create Mac, iPod, iPad, Apple Watch, and iPhone, while Bongiorno led teams as a Director of Software Engineering."}]}

Humane also stopped selling AI pins as of yesterday and canceled any orders that had been made but not yet fulfilled. Humane said it is discontinuing the AI Pin because it’s “moving onto new endeavors.”.","score":0.7982658,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.biometricupdate.com/202502/humane-is-bricking-ai-pins-following-116m-hp-buyout","title":"Humane is bricking AI Pins following $116M HP buyout","content":"Humane is shutting down and shuttering services for its AI Pin leaving owners with a bricked product.
